chunks.text: Narges Norouzi is an Associate Teaching Professor at UC Berkeley's EECS department. Her contact information includes 775 Soda Hall and email norouzi@berkeley.edu. She has a personal homepage, publications, and research support available through links.
chunks.question: What is Narges Norouzi's academic position?
chunks.answer: Associate Teaching Professor at UC Berkeley

chunks.text: Narges Norouzi earned her Ph.D. and M.A.Sc. in Electrical & Computer Engineering from the University of Toronto in 2017 and 2014 respectively. She completed her B.A.Sc. at Sharif University of Technology in 2012. Her research focuses on Computational Biology, Biometric Systems, Machine Learning, and Statistical Signal Processing.
chunks.question: Where did Narges Norouzi earn her Ph.D.?

chunks.text: Her research applications include Image Processing, Pattern Recognition, and Adaptive Systems. She is involved in research areas such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Education (EDUC), and Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO). She is affiliated with research centers like Algorithms and Computing for Education (ACE) and Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR).
chunks.question: What are Narges Norouzi's research applications?
chunks.answer: Image Processing, Pattern Recognition, Adaptive Systems

chunks.text: Narges Norouzi teaches courses including CS 194-202: Careers in Data Science, Computing, and Mathematical and Physical Sciences on Tuesdays 09:30-10:59 in Soda 438. She also teaches CS 194-271 and CS 294-271: Research in AI Education on Tuesdays 12:30-13:59 in Soda 606.
chunks.question: What courses does Narges Norouzi teach?
